frozen_components: language model weights (BERT, LLaMA, Pythia models)
architecture.summary: LEACE is a closed-form linear projection method for concept erasure from embeddings. It computes an affine transformation that removes linear information about a target concept (Y) from an embedding (X) by whitening X, projecting onto the nullspace of the cross-covariance matrix between X and Y, then unwhitening. The method is applied to LLMs via 'concept scrubbing': sequentially applying LEACE to activations at each layer, feeding scrubbed outputs forward to the next layer.

biblio.title: LEACE: Perfect linear concept erasure in closed form

claims.statement: A classification task is linearly guarded if and only if every class has exactly the same mean feature vector

LEACE is the unique affine transformation that minimizes mean squared distance from original features with respect to all norms induced by inner products (Euclidean, Mahalanobis, etc.) while achieving linear guardedness
LEACE achieves random chance accuracy (perfect erasure) on gender prediction from BERT embeddings with minimal edit distance, while RLACE comes close and INLP does not
LEACE is approximately 2 orders of magnitude faster than RLACE and does not require gradient-based optimization
Profession-prediction accuracy drops from 79.3% to 77.3% after gender erasure via LEACE on BERT embeddings; TPR-gap drops from 0.198 to 0.084

Erasing part-of-speech information via LEACE concept scrubbing induces perplexity increases of 1.73–3.57 bits per UTF-8 byte across LLaMA (7B–30B) and Pythia (160M–12B) models, compared to SAL which induces 3.16–4.69 bits
INLP requires 20 iterations to partially erase POS information and reduces embedding rank by 360 dimensions; LEACE reduces rank by only 17 dimensions
Concept scrubbing remains largely unvalidated on narrow concepts and behavioral metrics; it is unclear whether scrubbing will successfully prevent nonlinear encoding of protected attributes
